title: Eleocharis mohamadii
taxonomy: Eleocharis mohamadii is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family, Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Eleocharis, a group of plants commonly known as spikesedges or spikerushes. The classification of Eleocharis mohamadii is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade angiosperms, the clade monocots, and the order Poales. Within the family Cyperaceae, Eleocharis mohamadii is placed in the subfamily Cyperoideae. The species is also known by the synonyms of Trichophyllum mohamadii.

intro: Eleocharis mohamadii is a type of aquatic plant, specifically a sedge, belonging to the Cyperaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eleocharis mohamadii include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
